name: Team Gillum
subject: Happy Halloween from the Gillums!
body:
#
Friends -
Usually, it's Andrew sending these emails reminding you about our critical end-of-month fundraising deadlines, but tonight, we couldn't resist giving him a few hours off for daddy duty.
How could anyone say no to these kids?
But while Andrew's out trick-or-treating, we still need to make sure we're raising the funds we need to power this campaign, and win back the Governor's Office after over two decades of Republican and special interest control.
It looks like you haven't had the opportunity to chip in to our campaign online yet. [**Can we count on you to take this next step in supporting our campaign before midnight to ensure we have the resources we need to spread our message across the state?**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb327a/9e00690/4e7a3f81/2403110e/2338101253/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-oct2017?refcode=em171031-pm-nd")
We hope you have a happy and safe Halloween!
Bring it home,
Team Gillum

****[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb327a/9e00690/4e7a3f81/2403110e/2338101253/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-oct2017?refcode=em171031-pm-nd")****

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb327a/9e00690/4e7a3f81/24031101/2338101253/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). name: The White House
subject: American Resilience
body:
Your 1600 Daily:
| | [ ](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=87be737a45ab915cb8bbc1c2a1349e241c3d465cf10d3e97ad41608448a5e0967a75844fcbd332108c8ea4b2be40a2635dad708d2fc0d767) “Our thoughts and prayers are with the victims of yesterday's terrorist attack in New York City and their families. My Administration will provide its full support to the New York City Police Department, including through a joint investigation with the Federal Bureau of Investigation. We offer our thanks to the first responders who stopped the suspect and rendered immediate aid to the victims of this cowardly attack. These brave men and women embody the true American spirit of resilience and courage. I will continue to follow developments closely.” -President Donald J. Trump \------------------ **Hope for Historic Tax Reform by the Holidays**
On Tuesday, President Trump participated in a tax reform industry meeting in the Roosevelt Room with President and CEO of the U.S. Chamber of Commerce Tom Donohue and other business leaders. The President stated that he would like the House to pass a bill by Thanksgiving lowering taxes and cutting regulations and hopes to conduct the “biggest tax event in the history of our country” by adopting a new tax code before Christmas. To learn more about the Unified Framework for Tax Reform supported by the President, visit [www.whitehouse.gov/taxreform.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=87be737a45ab915cbc5a4aa2427343276425d19d6a8d59ad467c72a1202c8d3225c5ba8837cd3898f400b3849b16cd8736ab4a889495b7b0) Read the President’s full remarks before the meeting [here.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=87be737a45ab915c144931f4e135f94fc483ccb7d001c366b5c6999785fb88a6489216152afc81b77c451839272b8934dfc7e40d5fa2d740) **Vice President Attends Senate GOP Luncheon**
Vice President Mike Pence met with Republican Senators and Committee Chairs on Capitol Hill on Tuesday about delivering President Donald Trump's pro-jobs, pro-worker, pro-family tax reforms for hard working Americans. The Vice President talked about how he has traveled across the Nation, and at each stop, working families and job creators have told him the same thing: They said that the biggest obstacle to their success is our country's broken tax code. The Vice President made clear that President Trump is committed to signing the largest tax cut in American history in order to put more money in working Americans' pockets and to put our job creators back on the path to success. Read more about the Unified Framework for Tax Reform [here.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=87be737a45ab915c448be71cf1f09a9dff10aaf53666740e4ad686cef5a252084eed57c0469069a63de9a9a40b1dde7435678253e9ed1009) **Puerto Rico Finds Powerful Partner in FEMA**
Lieutenant Governor and Secretary of State of Puerto Rico Luis G. Rivera-Marín penned an op-ed in the October 30, 2017 edition of the Miami Herald. “Reviewing the aid provided by the federal government, specifically by FEMA, under the steady leadership of Administrator Brock Long, reveals that Washington’s response has and will be there for the U.S. citizens residing on the island,” Rivera-Marin wrote. He provided figures illustrating the scope of the federal response and expressed hope that Puerto Rico will thrive in the wake of disaster alongside its “federal brethren, who have been bearing the brunt and weathering the winds and rain next to us since Day One.” Read the full editorial [here.](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=87be737a45ab915cfdf270c7594dc4eb887950b37923cdf8c76af00754094d1135f6d4a175df0f7bca263cadcbb6734851dff972fed19aab)
# Photo of the Day
**President Donald J. Trump hosts a tax reform industry and business leaders meeting** | October 31, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)
# POTUS TODAY
Today, President Trump will receive his daily intelligence briefing and then hold a Cabinet meeting. In the afternoon, the President will sign the National Veterans and Military Families Month Proclamation. The President will then have lunch with Vice President Mike Pence, Secretary of State Rex Tillerson, and Secretary of Defense James Mattis. Later in the afternoon, the President will sign H.J. Res. 111 – Disapproving of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s Arbitration Agreements Rule. Vice President Pence will join the President for a Cabinet meeting and the signing of the National Veterans and Military Families Month Proclamation. In the afternoon, the Vice President will be visiting the headquarters of the CIA to participate in a series of briefings.
# COMING UP
On Friday, President Trump will depart on a trip to Japan, the Republic of Korea, China, Vietnam, and the Philippines. This will be the President’s fourth international trip since taking office and his first official visit to Asia. This trip will underscore the Administration’s commitment to longstanding United States alliances and partnerships, and reaffirm United States leadership in promoting a free and open Indo-Pacific region.
---

[Privacy Policy](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=87be737a45ab915cc778bef9112387092ea58ee7f883f45dc6871d229d4b35d97bded70e60302635811ae5078ec00ec3f4129a4b4e5e796e) | [Contact the White House](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=87be737a45ab915ce518d9806f931d9ca3aceb892eb14452cdd3edc5e25f4911e2d758ca0e5c3f047a12cd18da3d3e1fc77bd5132f19d7c5) | [Unsubscribe](https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d418d9cfbdc2444dfe91381100af26f11b13d47b2fcb40674246452c8e07e2a42b3264b2a4ac5a4672688fca08365a1e16)

Rivera-Marín penned an op-ed in the October 30, 2017 edition of the Miami Herald. “Reviewing the aid provided by the federal government, specifically by FEMA, under the steady leadership of Administrator Brock Long, reveals that Washington’s response has and will be there for the U.S. citizens residing on the island,” Rivera-Marin wrote. He provided figures illustrating the scope of the federal response and expressed hope that Puerto Rico will thrive in the wake of disaster alongside its “federal brethren, who have been bearing the brunt and weathering the winds and rain next to us since Day One.” Read the full editorial Photo of the Day President Donald J. Trump hosts a tax reform industry and business leaders meeting | October 31, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead) POTUS TODAY Today, President Trump will receive his daily intelligence briefing and then hold a Cabinet meeting. In the afternoon, the President will sign the National Veterans and Military Families Month Proclamation. The President will then have lunch with Vice President Mike Pence, Secretary of State Rex Tillerson, and Secretary of Defense James Mattis. Later in the afternoon, the President will sign H.J. Res. 111 – Disapproving of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s Arbitration Agreements Rule. Vice President Pence will join the President for a Cabinet meeting and the signing of the National Veterans and Military Families Month Proclamation. In the afternoon, the Vice President will be visiting the headquarters of the CIA to participate in a series of briefings. COMING UP On Friday, President Trump will depart on a trip to Japan, the Republic of Korea, China, Vietnam, and the Philippines. This will be the President’s fourth international trip since taking office and his first official visit to Asia. This trip will underscore the Administration’s commitment to longstanding United States alliances and partnerships, and reaffirm United States leadership in promoting a free and open Indo-Pacific region. | |

| | **_National Day of Action - Saturday, November 4th_**
Fellow Republicans,

We're pleased to announced that the Indiana Republican Party, in conjunction with our friends at the Republican National Committee, is bringing the popular and successful [Republican Leadership Initiative (RLI) training program](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3706625ec0&e=1acd9bb3d6) right here to Indiana beginning on Saturday, November 4 - the National Day of Action!

Throughout next week, we'll be visiting seven counties covering six Congressional Districts to train our most dedicated volunteers and activists on the latest technology and the most effective tools we have to organize neighborhoods, canvass voters and elect Republicans in 2018, 2020 and beyond.

This [EXCLUSIVE and FREE training program](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c6dc973abe&e=1acd9bb3d6) is run by tested Republican operatives with years of experience right here in Indiana and is open only to the most dedicated conservative Republican activists or those who want to get more involved.

---

Peter,

No matter what President Trump does, Republicans don't seem interested in holding him accountable.

This week, he's trying to shift blame away from members of his campaign team who have been **INDICTED** for coordinating with the Russian government.

But congressional Republicans led by Mitch McConnell and Paul Ryan seem more interested in passing a new tax cut for the rich.

If McConnell and Ryan won't do the right thing, we know people who will -- pro-choice Democratic women.

**[Donate $3 to help elect more pro-choice Democratic women to the House and Senate who will hold Trump accountable every step of the way.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6c5c/24c69fd9/174852151/189f537d/2323814510/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171101_email_NoMatterWhat?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Donate+3+to+help+elect+more+prochoice+De&utm_campaign=WQEFEK01N_digital_email_20171101_NoMatterWhat_inact&source=WQEFEK01N_digital_email_20171101_NoMatterWhat_inact") **

Even if Republicans know Trump's actions are wrong, they continue to ignore them and support his administration.

House Republicans investigating the Trump team's links with Russia during the election are reportedly trying to wrap up the proceedings quickly and quietly rather than getting the answers we deserve.

And Paul Ryan recently _joked_ about ignoring Trump's ugly rhetoric, saying, "Every morning I wake up in my office and I scroll through Twitter to see which tweets I will have to pretend I didn't see later on." Frankly, we're not laughing.

Every single American deserves better leadership than this. And we're going to make sure they get it -- by electing more leaders like Maxine Waters, Elizabeth Warren, Nancy Pelosi, Nydia Velazquez, and Frederica Wilson.

**Donate $3 to make sure Trump is held accountable by replacing the cowardly Republicans in Congress with our women:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Hold-Trump-Accountable](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6c5c/24c69fd9/174852151/189f537f/2323814510/VEsD/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171101_email_NoMatterWhat?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=5+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgHoldTrumpAccount&utm_campaign=WQEFEK01N_digital_email_20171101_NoMatterWhat_inact&source=WQEFEK01N_digital_email_20171101_NoMatterWhat_inact") **

Thank you,

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

If McConnell and Ryan won't do the right thing, we know people who will -- pro-choice Democratic women. "" Even if Republicans know Trump's actions are wrong, they continue to ignore them and support his administration. House Republicans investigating the Trump team's links with Russia during the election are reportedly trying to wrap up the proceedings quickly and quietly rather than getting the answers we deserve. And Paul Ryan recently joked about ignoring Trump's ugly rhetoric, saying, "Every morning I wake up in my office and I scroll through Twitter to see which tweets I will have to pretend I didn't see later on." Frankly, we're not laughing. Every single American deserves better leadership than this. And we're going to make sure they get it -- by electing more leaders like Maxine Waters, Elizabeth Warren, Nancy Pelosi, Nydia Velazquez, and Frederica Wilson. name: Lucinda Guinn, EMILY's List
subject: FWD: [Volunteers needed] Help our women win in Virginia >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6c52/24c69fd9/174bc5d7e/189f4ba0/4098394776/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEK03_digital_email_20171101_VAgeneralKick&source=WQENEK03_digital_email_20171101_VAgeneralKick")
---

Peter,

We are down to the wire.

Election Day in Virginia is less than one week away, and our women still need our help to win several incredibly close races.

This is your last chance to help their campaigns before Election Day.

**Sign up now to volunteer to help our women's campaigns turn out voters on November 7th:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Volunteer-in-Virginia](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6c52/24c69fd9/174bc5d7e/189f4ba1/4098394776/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20171026_email_VAgeneral?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgVolunteerinVirgi&utm_campaign=WQENEK03_digital_email_20171101_VAgeneralKick&source=WQENEK03_digital_email_20171101_VAgeneralKick")**

Thanks,

Lucinda Guinn
Vice President of Campaigns, EMILY's List

_\-----------Original Message----------
From: Lucinda Guinn, EMILY's List
Sent: Thurs, Oct 26, 2017 at 8:07 p.m._
Peter,

The Virginia election is just 12 days away, and we are ramping up to help our candidates there win.

Right now we have **16** endorsed women running, all for House of Delegates seats.

These races are expected to be close, so our women need a small army of volunteers to make sure voters who support them get to the polls.

**[Sign up to volunteer to help our women running in Virginia turn out critical votes they need to win on Election Day.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6c52/24c69fd9/174bc5d7e/189f4ba2/4098394776/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20171026_email_VAgeneral?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Sign+up+to+volunteer+to+help+our+women+r&utm_campaign=WQENEK03_digital_email_20171101_VAgeneralKick&source=WQENEK03_digital_email_20171101_VAgeneralKick")**

These are phenomenal candidates I guarantee you'll be proud to say you helped elect. Here are some of them:
There's **Danica Roem** , a reporter who, if elected, would be the first openly transgender state representative in the COUNTRY. She's running against a viciously anti-LGBTQ incumbent who authored the state's discriminatory "bathroom bill."

**Hala Ayala** , whose father was an immigrant from El Salvador, and **Elizabeth Guzman** , an immigrant from Peru, would be the first Latinas elected to the legislature.

And**Jennifer Carroll Foy** is a public defender who attended the Virginia Military Institute during one of the first years women were allowed to attend. She won her primary by 12 votes while being on bed rest on election night due to her pregnancy with twins.
These women, along with other EMILY's List-endorsed candidates, need us to show up if they're going to win. **And you showing up to help really does make a difference, Peter.**

A strong program to get out the vote can change turnout by up to five percent. In 2013, five of the closest races for the Virginia House of Delegates were less than two points apart -- and Republicans won four of them. If Democrats could have turned out a couple hundred more voters in those districts, they could have won.

I'm so excited to get down to Virginia to help make sure voters get to the polls. I hope you'll volunteer too to help our women win.

**Sign up to help our Virginia candidates get out the vote:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Volunteer-in-Virginia](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6c52/24c69fd9/174bc5d7e/189f4ba3/4098394776/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20171026_email_VAgeneral?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgVolunteerinVirgi&utm_campaign=WQENEK03_digital_email_20171101_VAgeneralKick&source=WQENEK03_digital_email_20171101_VAgeneralKick")**

Thanks,

Lucinda Guinn
Vice President of Campaigns, EMILY's List

|
